title: Assessment principle
text: The claim workflow should first classify the transport/consumer domain, then apply only the relevant legal instrument and thresholds for that domain.

title: EU261 delay threshold
text: Under Regulation (EC) No 261/2004 and related case law, compensation analysis is generally triggered when arrival delay is 3 hours or more, subject to extraordinary circumstances.

title: EU261 compensation levels
text: Typical compensation levels are EUR 250 (up to 1500 km), EUR 400 (many flights up to 3500 km), and EUR 600 (longer flights).

title: EU261 extraordinary circumstances
text: Air carrier may deny compensation if it proves extraordinary circumstances that could not have been avoided even with all reasonable measures.

title: Rail delay compensation 60+
text: Regulation (EU) 2021/782 provides compensation baseline of 25% of ticket price when arrival delay is between 60 and 119 minutes.

title: Rail delay compensation 120+
text: Regulation (EU) 2021/782 provides compensation baseline of 50% of ticket price when arrival delay is 120 minutes or more.

title: Bus/coach coverage threshold
text: For regular bus/coach services, key rerouting/refund protections generally apply to journeys of 250 km or more.

title: Bus/coach major disruption trigger
text: Cancellation or delay of at least 120 minutes for covered services can trigger re-routing or reimbursement rights.

title: Sea compensation principle
text: Regulation (EU) No 1177/2010 ties compensation to both scheduled voyage duration and length of arrival delay.

title: Sea compensation percentages
text: Compensation is typically 25% or 50% of ticket price depending on applicable delay band for the route duration.

title: Consumer delivery timing
text: Under Directive 2011/83/EU, trader should deliver by agreed date, or within 30 days if no date is agreed.

title: Consumer remedy for non-delivery
text: If trader does not deliver on time, consumer may set an additional period and terminate for refund if still undelivered.

title: Package travel refund right
text: Directive (EU) 2015/2302 can entitle travellers to refund after organiser cancellation or relevant termination events.

title: Package travel refund timeline
text: Where refund is due under package travel rules, payment is typically expected within 14 days after termination.
